# Mohit Ranka

> Engineering leader. Builder of data platforms, developer tooling, and reliable distributed backends.

Mohit Ranka is an engineering leader focused on data platforms, developer tooling, and reliable distributed backends. He prefers clarity over cleverness, long-term impact over short wins, and systems that scale from one team to many.

Experience highlights:
- LinkedIn: consolidated diverse data sources into a near-real-time platform
- Postman: led the web launch
- Booking.com: built identity and SSO systems

Location and background: India; B.Tech. from Vellore Institute of Technology.

Primary contact: mohitranka@gmail.com
Site: https://www.mohitranka.com

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.mohitranka.com/): About Mohit Ranka — background, focus areas, and selected writing
- [Writing](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog.html): Technical writing on platforms, data, reliability, and leadership
- [Contact](https://www.mohitranka.com/contact/): Email contact for architecture, reliability, or AI-system topics

## Writing

- [Near-real-time is a product promise, not a Kafka cluster](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/near-real-time-is-a-product-promise/): Freshness contracts vs streaming infrastructure
- [The platform team’s real job is interfaces](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/platform-teams-real-job-is-interfaces/): Platform leverage through stable boundaries
- [When I still choose a relational database](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/when-i-still-choose-a-relational-database/): Modern sequel on datastore judgment
- [Freshness SLOs: the metric product teams actually feel](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/freshness-slos-the-metric-product-teams-feel/): SLOs for age of truth in data platforms
- [Saying no as a platform EM without becoming the villain](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/saying-no-as-a-platform-em/): Prioritization and partner trust for platform leaders
- [Identity systems fail socially before they fail cryptographically](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/identity-systems-fail-socially/): Ownership, exceptions, and session semantics
- [What “led the web launch” taught me about constraints](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/web-launch-constraints/): Shipping a web surface under real constraints
- [Cost regressions are production incidents](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/cost-regressions-are-production-incidents/): Treating spend anomalies as operational events
- [How I review a distributed design in 45 minutes](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/how-i-review-a-distributed-design/): A practical architecture review checklist
- [AI features that deserve a data contract](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/ai-features-that-deserve-a-data-contract/): Contracts, evals, and ownership for AI products
- [RDBMS vs. NOSQL?](https://www.mohitranka.com/blog/rdbms-vs-nosql/): Earlier essay on when relational databases still win (2013)
